steps.title: Distribute to simplify the right side
steps.content:
Start with $y = \frac{1}{2}(15x + 12) + 3x$. Distribute $\frac{1}{2}$ across the parentheses:
$$y = \frac{1}{2}(15x) + \frac{1}{2}(12) + 3x = \frac{15}{2}x + 6 + 3x$$

steps.title: Combine x terms and identify the slope
steps.content:
Combine the $x$ terms: $\frac{15}{2}x + 3x = \frac{15}{2}x + \frac{6}{2}x = \frac{21}{2}x$
The simplified equation is $y = \frac{21}{2}x + 6$, which is in the form $y = mx + b$. The slope is $\frac{21}{2}$ or \$10.5$.
